name: Pyro (Deep Probabilistic Programming)
pricing_notes: Open source (Apache-2.0); Uber AI Labs / PyTorch ecosystem

performance.notes: PyTorch backend; GPU support; MCMC can be slow; SVI (variational) much faster

agent_readiness.notes: Deep probabilistic programming on PyTorch. import pyro. import pyro.distributions as dist. def model(data): mu = pyro.sample('mu', dist.Normal(0, 1)). sigma = pyro.sample('sigma', dist.HalfNormal(1)). with pyro.plate('obs', len(data)): pyro.sample('x', dist.Normal(mu, sigma), obs=data). Inference: SVI (stochastic variational), MCMC (NUTS, HMC). Auto-guides: AutoNormal, AutoMultivariateNormal. Neural network priors: combining nn.Module with probabilistic layers. Gaussian Processes. Deep generative models (VAE). NumPyro is a JAX-based alternative with better performance.

gotchas: Steep learning curve — requires probabilistic programming mental model
MCMC (NUTS) is slow vs SVI; need to choose inference algorithm carefully
NumPyro (JAX-based) is generally faster and increasingly preferred
Debugging shape errors in plate/dim notation requires expertise
best_when: Bayesian neural networks and deep generative models
Probabilistic models requiring PyTorch neural network components
Variational inference on complex hierarchical models
Research combining deep learning with probabilistic programming
avoid_when: Simple Bayesian models — PyMC more ergonomic
summary: Pyro is Uber AI's deep probabilistic programming library on PyTorch. pyro.sample('z', dist.Normal(0, 1)) defines latent variables. Inference: SVI (stochastic variational) or MCMC (NUTS). Auto-guides for amortized inference. Combines PyTorch nn.Module with probabilistic priors for Bayesian NNs and VAEs. Gaussian Processes. NumPyro (JAX-based) is faster and increasingly preferred. PyMC is more ergonomic for classical Bayesian analysis.
